fields.label: API key
fields.description: Placeholder secret. The Hello World datasource reads no configuration: its config editor renders static text and its backend ignores instance settings. This key exists only because a dsconfig entry must declare at least one field and the shared conformance suite requires at least one secureJsonData key. The plugin never reads it.

instructions.msg: The Hello World datasource is a minimal sample/template plugin with NO configuration surface. Its config editor (src/module.tsx:32-34) renders the static text 'Hello World Config Editor!' and never calls onOptionsChange, so it writes nothing to root, jsonData, or secureJsonData. The frontend config types are blank: `export type Config = {} & DataSourceJsonData` and `export type SecureConfig = {}` (src/module.tsx:13-14). The backend (pkg/main.go) ignores instance settings entirely — the datasource instance factory returns an empty DatasourceInstance{} without reading settings.JSONData, settings.URL, or any secret (pkg/main.go:40-51). Minimal working payload: {"jsonData":{}}. No URL, authentication, or secret is required or used.

The single secureJsonData.apiKey field in this schema is a PLACEHOLDER, not a real credential. Upstream declares no secrets (`SecureConfig = {}`, src/module.tsx:14); this key exists only because a dsconfig entry must declare at least one field (dsconfig Schema.Validate rejects an empty `fields` array with 'fields is required') and the shared conformance suite requires at least one secureJsonData key (schema.PluginUnderTest rejects empty SecureKeys; SchemaRoundTrip asserts SecureValues is non-empty). The Hello World backend never reads it — setting or omitting secureJsonData.apiKey has no effect on the datasource's behavior.

Behavior is static and does not depend on configuration. CheckHealth always returns OK with the message 'hello world datasource just works but does nothing' (pkg/main.go:17-22). QueryData returns a single-string frame ('hello world response') carrying the notice 'hello world query works. but not fully implemented' for every query (pkg/main.go:24-38). There is nothing to configure to change this; the plugin is a backend-datasource scaffold built on DataSourceWithBackend (src/module.tsx:11,18).
